title: Italy v Wales moved to Sunday over Pope's funeral
content:
Italy and Wales were originally scheduled to play in the final round of the tournament on Saturday

Women's Six Nations: Italy v Wales

Venue:Stadio Sergio LanfranchiDate:Sunday, 27 AprilKick-off:11:30 BST

Coverage:Watch on BBC One Wales, BBC iPlayer, BBC Sport online; listen on BBC Sounds, BBC Radio Wales and BBC Radio Cymru

The Women's Six Nations fixture between Italy and Wales has been switched to Sunday, 27 April (11:30 BST) because of the funeral of Pope Francis.

The teams were due to meet in Parma at 12:15 BST on Saturday, 26 April in the final round of the tournament.

But the match will now take place on the following day at the Stadio Sergio Lanfranchi, the same venue as originally planned.

The switch comes after the Vatican announced the Pope's funeral will take place at 09:00 BST on Saturday.

Italy's government hasdeclared five days of national mourningin the run-up to the funeral.

Hurt Wales coach Lynn needs 'good pre-season'

Ireland score six tries to ease past winless Wales

Tackling fires and rugby players - Scoble is living the dream

Speaking on Tuesday before the decision to move the game was made, Wales vice-captain Keira Bevan said the squad were preparing as normal.

The fixture switch is the latest disruption faced by Wales in what has been another disappointing campaign despite the appointment of new head coach Sean Lynn just before the tournament.

They faced a13-hour journey to Briveafter opting to travel to France on the eve of their match earlier this month, only to be caught up in congestion after a crash. The delay saw Wales miss their team run-through.

Wales have lost all four games in the competition and must beat Italy to have a chance to avoid finishing bottom of the table for the second year in a row.
